title: <name> and the Fairy by the River

pages.text: One sunny morning, <name> walked to the river near her village. The water sparkled like tiny stars, and butterflies danced above the lilies. She hummed a tune and picked a mango from the tree by the bank.
As she sat by the water, something shiny floated toward her — a tiny glowing feather! “How pretty!” <name> whispered. When she touched it, a soft voice giggled, “Follow me!”
The feather twirled through the air, leading her to a patch of flowers glowing like lanterns. There, sitting on a lotus leaf, was a tiny fairy with silver wings and a dress made of petals.
“Namaste, <name>,” said the fairy. “I am Neerja, the river fairy. Thank you for finding my magic feather!” <name> smiled. “Can you teach me some magic?”

The fairy nodded and waved her wand. “Sing with me!” Together they sang a soft, tinkly song — “Bloom and shine, laugh and grow, kindness makes the flowers glow!” As they sang, the garden bloomed — marigolds, roses, and jasmine opened in a rainbow of colors.

Birds chirped happily, and the air smelled sweet like honey. “You have a kind heart,” said Neerja. “Always share your smile — that’s real magic.” Before she disappeared, the fairy left a tiny glowing flower in <name>’s palm. It sparkled softly, even in daylight.
<name> ran home, heart shining as bright as the river.
